trade.summary: Moderate bilateral trade. Switzerland exports pharmaceuticals, machinery, and chemicals. South Africa exports precious metals (gold, platinum, diamonds), vehicles, and mineral products. Swiss commodity trading firms handle significant South African mineral exports.

diplomatic.summary: Stable bilateral relations. Switzerland has supported post-apartheid South Africa's development. Cooperation in multilateral forums including WTO and UN. South Africa's growing BRICS alignment creates some political divergence with Switzerland's Western-aligned neutrality. Some tension over South Africa's ICJ case against Israel.

military.summary: No military alliance or significant defense cooperation. Switzerland's neutrality precludes formal military ties. Some Swiss firms supply equipment to South African security forces.

financial.summary: Swiss banks manage significant South African private wealth. Swiss commodity trading companies (Glencore) are major players in South African mining sector. Some bilateral FDI in mining, pharmaceuticals, and consumer goods.

energy.summary: Limited direct energy trade. South Africa exports some coal through global markets. Switzerland has provided technical assistance for South Africa's renewable energy transition and nuclear power program.

scientific.summary: Moderate scientific cooperation. Swiss universities collaborate with South African institutions in infectious disease research, biodiversity, and materials science. Swiss government supports scientific capacity-building in South Africa.
